Piper Sandler (neutral, price target $76): Positive operating margins and a “competitive moat” make for a compelling story, but “valuation is a leap”
Growing importance of end-user experience should provide a tailwind, analyst Rob D. Owens writes in a note

Morgan Stanley (equal-weight, PT $67): Product-led growth model points to attractive and profitable opportunity, while the firm is seeing impressive adoption in the large enterprise market
That said, current valuation assumes success as an end-to-end platform, analyst Sanjit K Singh writes
KeyBanc (overweight, PT $86): Firm is uniquely positioned in the still-emerging DevOps market and could play a central role in scaling software development
